source: PPAC / Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas — approximate, dated reference (no clean free live feed exists)

crude.note: The Indian Basket is the crude mix India actually buys (sour Oman/Dubai + Brent). PPAC prices it daily; this is a recent reference level.

lpg.note: 14.2 kg domestic refill, metro reference price. PMUY (Ujjwala) beneficiaries get a per-cylinder subsidy.

priceMechanism: Since 2017 OMCs revise petrol/diesel daily in principle, but in practice pump prices move far less than crude — they tend to rise quickly when crude climbs and fall slowly when it drops, with tax absorbing much of the swing.
lastPriceRevisionNote: Last nationwide pump-price revision: a ₹2/litre cut on 14 Mar 2024. Prices had been frozen since May 2022 before that, and have barely moved since — even as crude swung.

ethanol.targetNote: 20% average ethanol blending in petrol (E20) — reached in 2025, about five years ahead of the 2030 target.
ethanol.mileageNote: E20 can lower fuel economy by roughly 1–6% in engines not tuned for it; newer E20-ready vehicles see less.
ethanol.why: Ethanol is blended into petrol mainly to cut crude imports and support cane/grain farmers.

taxRevenue.note: Approximate annual government revenue from petroleum taxes — central excise plus state VAT. One of the largest single revenue lines for both the Centre and the states.
